THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT - 1) PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS ARE THE OWNERS, AS TENANTS-IN-COMMON, OF THE SUBJECT OF THIS ACTION IN THE REAL PROPERTY COMMONLY KNOWN AS 2975 CESAR CHAVEZ STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CA; (2) THE OWNERS RESPECTIVE INTEREST IN THE REAL PROPERTY IS AS FOLLOWS:(A) SARINA NAVARRO - 16.666% (B) DIANNA LOPEZ - 16.666% (C) EDWARD LOPEZ - 16.666% (D) DEBORAH RIVERA - 16.666% (E) KEVIN LOPEZ - 16.666% (F) XIOMARA MEDINA - 8.333% (G) JAZZMIN MEDINA - 8.333% (THE INTEREST IN THE REAL PROPERTY OF JAZZMIN MEDINA IS SUBJECT TO THE LIEN OF HER ASSIGNEEES, INHERITANCE FUNDING COMPANY, INC. AND INHERITANCE LOAN COMPANY, LLC). (3) PLAINTIFF IS NOT AWARE OF ANY ADDITIONAL LIENS OR EMCUMBRANCES ON THE SUBJECT PREMISES ASIDE FROM THAT LISTED IN PARAGRAPH 2 ABOVE AND A LOAN FROM BANK OF AMERICA; (4) PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO SALE OF THE REAL PROPERTY AND DIVISION OF THE NET PROCEEDS OF SALE IS MORE EQUITABLE THAN DIVISION OF THE REAL PROPERTY IN KIND; (5) AMY HARRINGTON, ESQ. IS HEREBY APPLINTED REFEREE WITH THE AUTHORITY TO SELL THE PROPERTY A PUBLIC AUCTION TO THE HIGHEST BIDDER FOR CASH EXCEPT AS MA BE PROVIDED BY SECTIONS 873.600- 873.690 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE; (6) THE REFEREE SHALL HAVE THE AUTHORITY TO ENTER UPON THE REAL PROPERTY, TO INSPECT THE REAL PROPERTY, TO EVICT ANY PERSON OR PERSONS RESIDING IN THE REAL PROPERTY, TO BORROW MONEY TO REPAIR THE PROPERTY AND TO REPAY ANY MONEY BORROWED; (7) THE REAL PROPERTY SHALL BE SOLD ON AN AS BASIS, WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, EXCEPT AS TO TITLE; (8) THE REFEREE, AFTER SELLING THE REAL PROPERTY, SHALL REPORT TO THE COURT ON THE SALE PROCEEDINGS AND, UPON CONFIRMATION OF THE SALE BY THE COURT AND PAYMENT OF THE PURCHASE PRICE, IS AUTHORIZED TO EXECUTE AND DELIVER A DEED TO THE REAL PROPERTY TO THE PURCHASER; (9) THE PROCEEDS FROM THE SALE OF THE REAL PROPERTY SHALL BE APPLIED TO PAY THE FEES AND EXPENSES OF THE REFEREE AND HER ATTORNEY, IF ANY,TO PAY THE EXPENSES OF SALE, TO PAY THE COSTS OF PA

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2019 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Sarina Navarro, As Administrator Of The Estate Of Kenneth Lopez, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Interlocutory Judgment Prove Up Hearing For Default Interlocutory Judgment For Partition By Sale And Sale Of Real Property, And To Appoint Referee Against Defendants
Off Calendar May-07-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Kevin Lopez, Dianna Lopez, Deborah Rivera, Edward Lopez, Xiomara Medina and Jassmin Medina.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703. Email: mlyon@sftc.org.

Michael F. Woods, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Sarian Navarro, Administrator of hte Estate of Kenneth Lopez, Deceased, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Kevin Lopez, Dianna Lopez, Deborah Rivera, Edward Lopez, Xiomara Medina or Jassmin Medina.
Plaintiff's Amended Complaint for Partition of Real Property was filed on March 27, 2018.
A Lis Pendens was filed with the Clerk's Office on May 9, 2018.
Proof of Service of the Summons, Amended Complaint and Lis Pendens on defendants Edward Lopez and Dianna Lopez was filed on July 17, 2018.
Proof of Service of the Summons, Amended Complaint and Lis Pendens on defendant Jazzmin Medina was filed on July 20, 2018.
Proof of Service of the Summons, Amended Complaint and Lis Pendens on defendants Xiomara Medina and Deborah Rivera was filed on August 20, 2018.
Proof of Service of the Summons, Amended Complaint and Lis Pendens on defendant Kevin Lopez was filed on August 23, 2018.
Defaults as to defendants Dianna Lopez, Deborah Rivera, Edward Lopez, Xiomara Medina and Jassmin Medina.
Default as to defendant Kevin Lopez was entered on October 29, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Sarina Navarro, Administrator of hte Estate of Kenneth Lopez, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendants Kevin Lopez, Dianna Lopez, Deborah Rivera, Edward Lopez, Xiomara Medina and Jassmin Medina, as follows:

(1) Plaintiff and defendants are the owners, as tenants in common, of the subject of this action in the real property commonly known as 2975 Cesar Chavez Street, San Francisco, CA;
(2) The owners respective interest in the Real Property is as follows:(A) Sarina Navarro - 16.666% (B) Dianna Lopez - 16.666% (C) Edward Lopez - 16.666% (D) Deborah Rivera - 16.666% (E) Kevin Lopez - 16.666% (F) Xiomara Medina - 8.333% (G) Jazzmin Medina - 8.333% (the interest in the Real Property of Jazzmin Medina is subject to the lien of her assigneees, Inheritance Funding Company, Inc. and Inheritance Loan Company, LLC).
(3) Plaintiff is not aware of any additional liens or emcumbrances on the subject premises aside from that listed in paragraph 2 above and a loan from Bank of America;
(4) Plaintiff is entitled to Sale of the Real Property and division of the net proceeds of sale is more equitable than division of the Real Property in kind;
(5) Amy Harrington, Esq. is hereby applinted referee with the authority to sell the property a public auction to the highest bidder for cash except as ma be provided by Sections 873.600- 873.690 of the Code of Civil Procedure;
(6) The Referee shall have the authority to enter upon the Real Property, to inspect the Real Property, to evict any person or persons residing in the Real Property, to borrow money to repair the property and to repay any money borrowed;
(7) The Real Property shall be sold on an as basis, without any warranty, express or implied, except as to title;
(8) The Referee, after selling the Real Property, shall report to the Court on the sale proceedings and, upon confirmation of the sale by the Court and payment of the purchase price, is authorized to execute and deliver a deed to the Real Property to the purchaser;
(9) The proceeds from the sale of the Real Property shall be applied to pay the fees and expenses of the referee and her attorney, if any,to pay the expenses of sale, to pay the costs of partition or to secure any cost of partition later allowed, and to reimburse plaintiff Sarina Navarro for the cost of the litigation.

Proposed Interlocutory Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
